title: Adas Polo (Lentil Rice)
description: A delightful mix of fluffy basmati rice, earthy lentils, golden raisins, dates, and fragrant spices, often served with caramelized onions and ground beef.

instructions.text: Wash the basmati rice and soak in water. In a separate pot, cook the brown lentils in water until tender but not mushy. Drain.
In a skillet, heat 1 tbsp of oil and sauté the thinly sliced onion until golden and caramelized. Remove half for garnish.
In the same skillet, cook the ground beef with salt, pepper, and cinnamon until browned. Stir in the raisins and pitted dates just to warm through. Set aside.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil, add the soaked rice, and parboil for 5-7 minutes. Drain.
Add remaining oil and butter to the bottom of the pot. Layer the parboiled rice and the cooked lentils alternately.
Pour the bloomed saffron over the top layer. Cover with a towel-wrapped lid and steam on low heat until the rice is fluffy.
Serve the rice on a platter, topped with the beef, raisin, date mixture, and reserved caramelized onions.

tips: Be careful not to overcook the lentils during the boiling stage; they will continue to soften while steaming with the rice.
storage_notes: Keeps well in the fridge for up to 4 days. The flavors actually improve the next day.
serve_with: Mast-o-Khiar (yogurt cucumber dip) and Sabzi Khidan.

substitutions.note: Easily made vegan/vegetarian by omitting the meat.
Can provide a different but delicious sweet contrast.
